text: The surgeon observes pericholecystic edema and fluid around the gallbladder, with the infundibulum appearing adhered and difficult to manipulate.
The surgeon uses small hooks on the backside of the gallbladder to loosen the infundibulum, improving visibility of the surrounding structures.

The surgeon uses ICG dye to confirm the identification of the cystic artery on the right and the cystic duct on the left, both of which light up clearly. The critical view of safety is achieved, providing a clear image of the triangle of Calot.
The surgeon performs a quick check for the posterior artery, which remains slightly illuminated by the ICG dye, confirming its location.
